channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Robot Zuckerberg shows how IT can free up CEOs’ time
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg, the CEO of Meta, is building an AI version of himself. The virtual CEO is being trained on Zuckerberg’s mannerisms and will be loaded with his views on corporate strategy, the Financial Times reported.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: They bought property in the metaverse. Then it collapsed
item.description: Five years ago, tech angel investor Chris Adamo and a few friends jumped on a burgeoning trend in the digital asset world: they used a virtual real estate broker to buy 23 parcels of property in a metaverse called The Sandbox.
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: Artemis II astronauts praise their moonship's performance, especially the heat shield
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts who ignited a lunar renaissance are giving high marks to their moonship for its performance during reentry — especially the heat shield.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: AI shifts IT roles from operator to orchestrator
item.description: A new report from SolarWinds finds that artificial intelligence is reshaping IT roles, with 80% of professionals reporting a shift away from hands-on operations toward overseeing automated systems and workflows.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google DeepMind’s Demis Hassabis on the long game of AI
item.description: In 1988, a London pre-teen with a penchant for programming and gaming wrote a version of the classic board game Othello—also known as Reversi—for his Amiga 500 home computer. Teaching a piece of software to play the game was an ambitious coding project for someone so young.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: The agent tier: Rethinking runtime architecture for context-driven enterprise workflows
item.description: Most large enterprises run on deterministic software foundations. Business rules are embedded within workflows, state transitions are modeled explicitly and escalation paths are defined in advance. System behavior is specified in advance, making outcomes predictable.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Blancco confirms Mac adoption is accelerating
item.description: While sales of new Macs are surging the second-user market is also seeing strong momentum, prompting Blancco Technology Group and Cambrionix to introduce a new solution to help quickly erase and prepare large numbers of Macs for sale.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How a retired cranberry bog helped change the game for wetland restoration
item.description: The largest restored wetlands in Massachusetts now cover hundreds of acres of what used to be cranberry farms.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Jiang And Julia Vaz Of The Mit Graduate Program In Science Writing, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Roblox adds age-based accounts for kids and teens
item.description: Roblox is rolling out Kids and Select account types in early June, building separate environments for different age groups with built-in safety controls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2026 network outage report and internet health check
item.description: ThousandEyes, a Cisco company, monitors how ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services are handling any performance challenges and provides Network World with a weekly roundup of events that impact service delivery.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The hyperscalers are pricing themselves out of AI workloads
item.description: Large cloud providers still want the market to believe that AI infrastructure is a premium business where customers pay premium prices.
item.dc:creator: Info World
